meta.setting: Clear Creek within Wintu homeland near French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in community life, land pressure, and rugged 1899 work

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek Wintu homeland, villages, acorns, salmon, seasonal movement, fire stewardship, and Gold Rush disruption.

research_grounding.title: Redding Rancheria
research_grounding.use: Living Pit River, Wintu, and Yana community context, sovereignty, culture, families, and traditional homelands.

image_prompt.subject: Wintu family members and neighbors near Clear Creek doing practical camp and food work: baskets, salmon gear, acorn sacks, a horse pack, and a small controlled-burn smoke line far in the background
image_prompt.scene: autumn afternoon by oak and creek; everyday 1899 work clothing suited to weather and labor, children and elders present as people, not symbols, with a freight road visible across the water
image_prompt.composition: square respectful community scene with no ceremonial posing; creek and oak trees share the frame with people working, talking, watching the road, and keeping their own space
image_prompt.camera: human-height documentary game plate, slightly pulled back to give personal space and avoid trophy-like framing
material_cues: controlled-burn smoke far away, small and practical
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the dignity come from ordinary competence, kinship, and the fact that this is their place. Do not theatricalize anyone.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD community/environment scene
render_traits: slightly simplified but respectful human silhouettes
modest texture resolution on basketry, cloth, oak bark, water, and canvas
hard ambient occlusion under trees, packs, stones, and figures
warm autumn daylight with cool creek reflection
believable 1899 work clothing and practical objects
style_modifiers.palette: oak gold, creek blue, earth umber, smoke gray, work-cloth brown, faded red cloth accents, pine green, and warm late sun.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, writing, or invented Native text. Any road papers must be indistinct.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
cropped faces or isolated trophy portrait

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The people, creek, work objects, road, and oak setting are all fully readable.
Native presence is ordinary, respectful, community-centered, and grounded in place.
No invented sacred detail, fake language, costume spectacle, or modern object appears.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
